title: A novel model of appendicitis and appendectomy to investigate inflammatory bowel disease
author: Cheluvappa
materials: 50 ml of sterile Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS)
Sterile saline (Sodium Chloride Injections BP 0.9% tubes)

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (1) In mice, the major caecal lymphoid patch is the equivalent of the human appendix (Figure 1). In our AA model, the appendiceal pathology closely resembles that of human appendicitis; and AA offeres an age-, bacteria- and antigen-dependent protection against TNBS-colitis [22].

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (2) Balb/c mice (Male, 5 weeks), are intraperitonealy anaesthetised with xylazine (5mg/kg; Sigma-Aldrich, X1251) and ketamine (100 mg/kg; Sigma-Aldrich, K1884) followed by allocation into 2 treatment groups, the appendicitis group or the sham surgery group [23].

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: • Other permutations (appendicitis only or appendectomy only) do not ameliorate colitis pathology, and hence are excluded.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: • Mice are randomised to have either appendicitis or sham operation.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (3) Appendicitis is induced by constructing an appendiceal pouch from the caecal lymphoid patch, which is the equivalent of the human appendix.••This appendiceal pouch (Figure 1) is obstructed by rubber band ligation using standardised negative aspiration.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (4) Sham surgery entails a similar procedure, but without continuous obstruction by band ligation of the caecal patch.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: • It also involves the placement of a sterile rubber band in the abdominal cavity as a control for foreign body reaction.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: • While most human ulcerative colitis patients would not have undergone any form of abdominal surgery at diagnosis, it is essential that we control for the colonic and peritoneal milieu changes induced by a simple laparotomy in our experimental AA mice group.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: • Additionally, the small rubber ring used to encircle the main caecal follicle in the AA group needs to be controlled for too. Hence, in each SS group mouse, a rubber ring is introduced in the peritoneal cavity via a small laparotomy incision.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (5) Seven days following initial surgery, appendicitis mice undergo appendectomy (appendicitis and appendectomy, AA, group) by ligation and excision, while sham mice undergo a second sham surgery (sham and sham, SS, group).

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (6) All mice are monitored daily.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (7) Distal colons are harvested (3 days or 14 days or 28 days post-AA) without the recta - a 1 cm-segment (from the anus) are excluded prior to harvesting the distal colon.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (8) Transmural distal colonic segments are chosen to minimise artifactual changes and maximise pathophysiological relevance; as not only superficial mucosal tissue, but also colonic lymphoid nodules/aggregations and other tissue may “transmit” the protective immunological changes induced by appendicitis and appendectomy to the distal colon.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: • The proximal colon would be much more likely to be affected by the inflammatory changes experimentally induced by AA, owing to the close anatomical proximity of the main mouse caecal patch (≈human appendix) to the proximal colon.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: • The novelty of our study is that the most distal regions of the large gut sustain major persistent immunological changes (protective against colitis), by manipulation at the caecum, the most proximal region of the large gut.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (9) The processing of transmural distal colonic samples consists of flushing out of faecal contents with normal saline and immediately transfer to TRIzol® reagent (50-75 mg of tissue in 600 μL of TRIzol® reagent; Invitrogen Australia Pty Limited, 15596-026), snap-freezing in liquid nitrogen, and storing at -80°C until the microarray analysis.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (10) After chloroform and isopropanol treatment and centrifugation followed by washing the resultant pellet with 75% ethanol, air-drying and final re-constitution in nuclease-free H2O is done.

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (11) Concentration and purity of RNA is determined by automated optical density evaluation (OD 260/OD 280 ≥ 1.8 and OD 260/OD 230 ≥ 1.8) using Nanodrop ND-1000 (Nanodrop Technologies, Wilmington, DE, USA).

steps.title: Procedure overview and rationale minutiae - mouse model of appendicitis and appendectomy, and tissue processing
steps.instruction: (12) The degree of RNA degradation is analyzed by the Agilent electrophoresis bioanalyzer 2100 (Agilent Technologies Inc, Santa Clara, CA, USA) with the RNA integrity number (RIN) values consistently above 7.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (1) Shave a circumscribed area of each mouse’s abdomen (left side) a day before surgery.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (2) Weigh each mouse and make identification marks on each mouse’s tail a day before surgery.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (3) Wrap each P1000 pipette with sterile plastic sheet (from dressing pack).

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (4) Anaesthetise mouse with ketamine/xylazine/sterile PBS – Intraperitoneal injection of 0.1 ml of anaesthetic stock solution per 20 g mouse weight.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (5) Prepare surgical area observing sterile conditions meticulously:

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: • Use sterile gloves throughout.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: • Cover bench with sterile plastic sheet and tape the corners on the hood bench.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: • Use sterile plastic forceps to pick up the apparatus from the pack and put aside.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: • Load autoclaved equipment onto sterile plastic sheet by the “no touching” method.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: • Load bands, and pour Povidone-iodine onto sterile tray.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (6) Load band onto bevelled pipette tip with fine forceps and set aside.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (7) Clean surgical platform with ethanol and place on bench.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (8) Fix mouse to the platform by taping their arms/legs with taps.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (9) Moist eyes with a drop of saline to prevent xerosis and keratitis.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (10) Wipe abdomen with ethanol and clean the left part of the shaved abdominal wall with Povidone-iodine.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (11) Cover mouse with the sterile draper from dressing pack and make a hole in the draper above surgical area with fine forceps.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (12) Perform a ~1cm laparotomy on the left sided abdomen, using fine forceps to pick up skin and peritoneum.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (13) Mobilise caecum out to the surface with blunt curved forceps.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (14) Frequent moistening of the caecum with PBS is essential.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (15) Identify the 2 to 4 white appendiceal lymphoid patches, with one being far bigger than the others – this would be the one to do the procedure on.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (16) Attach bevelled pipette tip with band to pipette, and aspirate the lymphoid patch with firm suction pressure in gentle, but firm circular motion. Maintain suction pressure for 10 seconds before slowly unloading the band (with fine forceps) onto the neck of the newly created pouch.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: • Include some colonic tissue during suction because the created pouch will get smaller as each mouse ages.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (17) Using a curved forceps carefully return bowel into abdominal cavity by gently grasping the band.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (18) Dispense 1ml of PBS in the abdominal cavity to moisten the peritoneum.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (19) Suture the abdominal wall and the skin in layers.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (20) Give 1 ml normal saline subcutaneously (scruff of neck) after the procedure.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (21) Place mice on their side and put them close to each other in the cage to prevent hypothermia.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (22) Check post-op recovery in 1 hour for complete recovery. Check recovery for 1 week post operation and record observations in post operative period table.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (23) Appendectomy is performed by 7 days after appendicitis induction by gentle separation of adhesions, ligation, and excision of the inflamed area distal to the ligature.

steps.title: Procedure steps
steps.instruction: (24) The mice are sacrificed 3 days or 14 days or 28 days after that for distal colon harvesting.
